# wity-scene

A headless, isomorphic XML scene-graph library for spatial overlays and compositing. Deterministic: f(scene, t) → ComputedFrame. Zero runtime dependencies. Runs identically in browser and Node.js.

Current version: scene-core v1.0.0 · scene v1.0.0
Docs: https://www.wity.ai/stack/scene-graph/

## Architecture

Single core package with a thin re-export wrapper.

```
@wity/scene-core   — parse · evaluate · serialize · validate. Zero DOM (DOMParser is platform API).
        ↓
@wity/scene        — convenience re-export of scene-core
```

Import path:
- `import { parse, evaluate, serialize, validate, resolveUnit } from '@wity/scene-core'`

Core model: a scene document is a pure function of time. Call `evaluate(scene, t)` at any frame — no mutation, no playback state.

## Quick start

```js
import { parse, evaluate } from '@wity/scene-core';

const scene = parse(`
  <wity-scene version="1.0" width="1920" height="1080" dur="6.0">
    <ws-layer id="title" z="10">
      <ws-text x="50%" y="40%" anchor="center"
               font-size="5%" color="#ffffff"
               animate-in="fade-up" animate-dur="0.6">
        Opening Night
      </ws-text>
    </ws-layer>
  </wity-scene>
`);

const frame = evaluate(scene, 1.0);  // ComputedFrame at t=1s

for (const el of frame.elements) {
  if (el.visible) console.log(el.tag, el.x, el.y, el.content);
}
```

---

## Documentation pages

### Guides
- [Overview](/guide/overview) — core model, document structure, rendering targets
- [Schema v1.0](/guide/schema) — full XML attribute reference for all element types
- [Rendering](/guide/rendering) — HTML/CSS and Canvas 2D renderer examples, ComputedFrame shape

### API Reference
- [@wity/scene-core](/packages/scene-core) — parse, evaluate, serialize, validate, resolveUnit, all types
- [@wity/scene](/packages/scene) — re-export package
